title: Patriots 2026 position preview: Running backs edition
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Rhamondre Stevenson and TreVeyon Henderson lead a Patriots' backfield that ranked sixth in NFL rushing yards per game last year.
Rookie TreVeyon Henderson set a Patriots' record with 5.1 yards per carry, despite postseason struggles averaging 2.5 yards per carry.
Patriots' running back depth is a concern with limited offseason additions, relying heavily on Stevenson and Henderson's performance.

title: Cyclist hit and killed by vehicle on Tremont Street
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A cyclist was fatally struck by a vehicle near Roxbury Crossing on Tremont Street Thursday morning at 8:20 a.m.
The driver stopped after the accident; the victim was pronounced dead at the scene, according to Boston police.
Tremont Street closed from Brigham Circle to Roxbury Crossing until noon; BPD Homicide Unit is investigating.

title: Massachusetts Senate passes bill targeting addictive social media features for kids
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts Senate passed a bill to limit addictive social media features for minors with a 38-2 vote.
Bill requires disabling features like infinite scroll and autoplay for users under 18, and limits late-night notifications.
The legislation emphasizes protecting minors' privacy and balances safety with constitutional rights to access social media.

title: Police seize dog who severely injured smaller pup in Chelsea attack caught on video
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Chelsea police seized and quarantined a dog involved in a Monday night attack on Chestnut Street that injured a smaller pup.
The larger dog was determined to be unlicensed and will remain in quarantine for 10 days, as required by state law.
The smaller dog, a service animal named Snoopy Snoop Dog, needs a leg amputation following the attack, per WCVB.

title: Mac Jones opens up about his tumultuous time with the Patriots
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Mac Jones was traded from the New England Patriots to the Jacksonville Jaguars for a sixth-round pick.
Jones discussed challenges during his Patriots tenure, including shifting offensive leadership under Bill Belichick.
Jones was benched for Bailey Zappe in 2023 after reportedly seeking outside advice, before being traded to Jacksonville.

title: 5 takeaways as France defeat Morocco in the last ‘Boston Stadium’ World Cup game, advance to semifinals
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: France defeated Morocco 2-0 in the World Cup quarterfinals at Gillette Stadium, advancing to the semifinals.
Goals by Kylian Mbappe and Ousmane Dembélé secured the win for France against a strong defensive Moroccan side.
The match marked the final World Cup game at Gillette Stadium as Boston's hosting of the tournament concluded.

title: ‘He hit me’: Pregame France vs. Morocco press conference briefly halted after journalist altercation
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: A press conference in Boston for the France vs. Morocco World Cup match was interrupted by a journalist altercation.
FIFA officials intervened when a reporter accused another of hitting him during the conference at Gillette Stadium.
Moroccan player Brahim Díaz joked about forgetting the question after the incident disrupted the press conference.

title: After long search, scientists get amazing great white shark footage off R.I. coast
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Scientists filmed an eight-foot great white shark near a whale carcass southeast of Block Island in late June.
The Atlantic Shark Institute's Jon Dodd and Mystic Aquarium's Sarah Callan searched over 50 square miles to find the whale.
Rhode Island has had numerous noteworthy shark sightings this summer, including a beached porbeagle and thresher shark.

title: Iconic Christmas Tree Shops signs are headed to auction
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Two 27-foot Christmas Tree Shops signs from Hyannis will be auctioned starting at $1,000.
Proceeds from the auction will benefit Habitat for Humanity of Cape Cod.
The former store site in Hyannis is being transformed into a Best Buy, opening July 31.

title: A moment of appreciation for dominant Red Sox ace Sonny Gray
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Sonny Gray boasts a 10-1 record and a 2.61 ERA for the Boston Red Sox as of July 2026.
Despite his strong performance, Gray was not selected for the All-Star team; Ranger Suárez was chosen instead.
The Atlanta Braves have expressed interest in acquiring Gray amid the Red Sox's recent surge into playoff contention.
